perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's, [***] (the "Facilities"). [***] For the purposes of this paragraph, [***] facilities located at [***] are considered one Facility.
perturbation.changed_text: The Manufacturer shall produce the products described on Schedule A attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Deliverables"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's, [***] (the "Locations"). [***] For the purposes of this paragraph, [***] facilities located at [***] are considered one Location.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Products' to 'Deliverables' and 'Facilities' to 'Locations' introduces ambiguity. The contract then uses 'Products' and 'Facilities' in later sections, creating confusion about whether these terms are interchangeable with 'Deliverables' and 'Locations'. This is especially contradictory where 'Products' and 'Facilities' are explicitly defined elsewhere, potentially voiding obligations for items or places not initially intended.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Heritage and Premier agree that all Products subject to this Agreement, and their current and subsequently modified respective formulas are confidential and proprietary, and the sole property of Premier unless otherwise agreed in writing by both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'Products' with 'Deliverables' and 'Facilities' with 'Locations'. The contradicted text refers to 'Products' as subject to the agreement and defines their formulas as confidential. If 'Products' and 'Deliverables' are not the same, then the confidentiality clause may not apply to what is being delivered. This introduces ambiguity and potential contradiction, since what Premier considers confidential may not be protected if it is classified as a deliverable, and not a product, according to the contract. This adheres to the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All shipments of the Products shall be by common carrier, F.O.B. the Heritage [***] Facility or the Jasper Facility, as indicated by Premier in the Purchase Order.
perturbation.changed_text: All shipments of the Deliverables shall be by common carrier, F.O.B. the Heritage [***] Site or the Jasper Site, as indicated by Premier in the Purchase Order.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the term 'Facility' to 'Site', a divergence is created that causes misinterpretation regarding where the shipment of the deliverable should take place. While the original text explicitly states that the product will be shipped from the 'Facility', and the 'Site' is mentioned, this will lead to confusion on where the products should be shipped from. This perturbation creates an ambiguity regarding the specific destination for shipments, impacting logistical responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's, [***] (the "Facilities"). [***] For the purposes of this paragraph, [***] facilities located at [***] are considered one Facility.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies shipments F.O.B. the 'Facility,' defined in the contradicted text as Heritage's or Jasper's production locations ('Facilities'). The changed text replaces 'Facility' with 'Site,' creating ambiguity because the term 'Site' is not consistently defined as the production location. This misaligned terminology introduces an in-text contradiction by causing uncertainty regarding where the deliverable shipment originates, impacting the intended meaning and logistical responsibilities outlined in the original 'Facility' definition. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, where using 'multiple terms interchangeably without defining them' leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party recognizes that in the performance of this Agreement, it may acquire, directly or indirectly from the other Party, proprietary, confidential, trade secret, or information that is not otherwise available to the general public ("Confidential Information"). Each Party shall maintain control of all Confidential Information it receives and not disclose it or use it for any other purpose other than to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party recognizes that in the performance of this Agreement, it may acquire, directly or indirectly from the other Party, proprietary, confidential, trade secret, or data that is not otherwise available to the general public ("Private Data"). Each Party shall maintain control of all Private Data it receives and not disclose it or use it for any other purpose other than to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Switching 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Data' creates a contradiction. The term 'Confidential Information' is used again later in the same clause in a specific context. This change creates ambiguity and potential disputes on the scope and obligations of the 'Confidentiality' clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party recognizes that in the performance of this Agreement, it may acquire, directly or indirectly from the other Party, proprietary, confidential, trade secret, or information that is not otherwise available to the general public ("Confidential Information"). Each Party shall maintain control of all Private Data it receives and not disclose it or use it for any other purpose other than to perform its obligations under this Agreement. Each Party shall return the Private Data, along with all materials derived therefrom, to the disclosing Party upon demand or, destroy them and provide verification of destruction upon the termination of this Agreement at the request of the disclosing Party. Each Party acknowledges that the value of the other Party's Private Data is unique and substantial, and it may be impractical or difficult to assess its value in monetary terms. Accordingly, in the event of an actual or potential violation of this paragraph, the violating Party expressly consents to the enforcement of this Agreement by injunctive relief or specific performance in addition to any and all other remedies available to them. The Parties also agree to treat the terms and conditions of this Agreement as Private Data. The term Private Data shall not apply to portions of the Confidential Information that Party receiving it can show: (i) are or become generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the receiving Party; (ii) are in the receiving Party's possession from a source (other than the furnishing Party) that is not prohibited from disclosing such information, (iii) was known to the receiving Party prior to disclosure thereof by the furnishing Party; or (iv) are independently developed by the receiving Party without the use of any non-public, confidential or proprietary information received from the furnishing Party. A Party shall be entitled to disclose the other Party's Private Data as required pursuant to judicial action, governmental regulations or investigation, or other requirements. Such Party shall, to the extent allowed or permitted by the applicable judicial action, governmental regulation or investigation or other requirements, promptly notify the Party that furnished the Private Data prior to any such disclosure, and reasonably cooperate (at the request and expense of the furnishing Party) with the furnishing Party to contest or limit such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Data'. The contradicted text uses both 'Confidential Information' and 'Private Data', defining both separately but then using them inconsistently. Specifically, the definition section uses 'Confidential Information' while seemingly discussing 'Private Data' exceptions, leading to ambiguity and contradicting the intent of either definition, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The original text kept consistent use of 'Confidential Information', which then the contradicted text continues.
